title: Automation of status change of subitem greyed out when changing status of parent
excerpt: I am having issues trying to get a Subitem status to change when the parent item status changes. I has a error message when setting up the automation - ‘previous selection don’t include subitem context’ so the subitem st…

posts.author: andyoungy (Andy Young)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am having issues trying to get a Subitem status to change when the parent item status changes. I has a error message when setting up the automation - ‘previous selection don’t include subitem context’ so the subitem status column I want to update is greyed out? It feels like something Monday.com should be able to do, when a parent item is done then mark the sub items as done?

Screenshot 2022-06-09 at 16.40.542074×1046 190 KB

Anyone been able to do this or find a work around, or is this a bug?

posts.author: jxlmonday (Josh Li)
posts.cookedHtml: I am having the same issue, and cannot seem to find a workaround for it. Just wondering if anyone has found a solution yet.

posts.author: Ezimachia (Katherine McNarie)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was trying to use:

“When a status changes to something, change another status to something”

Every time I tried to set it up, the Subitem status would be grayed out. Then I tried selecting the 2nd status of the sentence (subitem) FIRST, then going back and choosing the 1st status. I wonder if that will work for you.

posts.author: MauritsNWC (MauritsNWC)
posts.cookedHtml:
We really want this feature as well.

For now they want you to use a paid app (free for 250 uses a month, 800euro a year for the 1st paid tier) to do this basic functionallity.

Reason its not working is that every subitem of a parent item is actually on another (hidden) board. This is why other automations also dont work with subitems. Seems like a design flaw in the backend to me.
